Prefer canonical first-party docs/pricing/auth pages. Read the actual page content and return the exact URLs visited plus concise evidence-backed findings. If docs are thin or JS-rendered, also try the main official website for pricing or product pages.

raw_result.description: Web scraping platform with an API for running scrapers and retrieving structured data results.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs show authentication with an API token passed in the `x-api-token` header; the browser run also noted analytics requests may use query parameters, but only token-based auth was evidenced.
raw_result.gating_reason: The official pricing page shows a self-serve Free plan with 1,000 tokens and 5 concurrent requests, indicating API access is available without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs describe a REST API with documented areas including scraper operations, results, analytics, and PDP cache/vertical-specific endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The official docs navigation includes an MCP Server integration page, indicating an existing official MCP offering, but the page content itself was not successfully verified in this pass.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official error-handling docs indicate HTTP 429 responses when rate limits are exceeded and mention a `Retry-After` header. The pricing page also exposes plan-level concurrency limits, including 5 concurrent requests on the Free plan.
raw_result.notable_signals: The docs appear broad and productized, covering auth, API overview, and error handling, and the pricing page exposes usage tokens and concurrency limits suitable for self-serve onboarding.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field `api_surface.mcp_exists` is not fully supported by a successfully read MCP documentation page in this pass; it was inferred from the official docs navigation and browser memory rather than directly verified from page content.

result.description: Web scraping platform with an API for running scrapers and retrieving structured data results.
result.auth_notes: Official API docs show authentication with an API token passed in the `x-api-token` header; the browser run also noted analytics requests may use query parameters, but only token-based auth was evidenced.
result.gating_reason: The official pricing page shows a self-serve Free plan with 1,000 tokens and 5 concurrent requests, indicating API access is available without sales approval.

api_surface.breadth: Official docs describe a REST API with documented areas including scraper operations, results, analytics, and PDP cache/vertical-specific endpoints.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The official docs navigation includes an MCP Server integration page, indicating an existing official MCP offering, but the page content itself was not successfully verified in this pass.

result.rate_limit_notes: Official error-handling docs indicate HTTP 429 responses when rate limits are exceeded and mention a `Retry-After` header. The pricing page also exposes plan-level concurrency limits, including 5 concurrent requests on the Free plan.
result.notable_signals: The docs appear broad and productized, covering auth, API overview, and error handling, and the pricing page exposes usage tokens and concurrency limits suitable for self-serve onboarding.
result.human_review_reason: The structured field `api_surface.mcp_exists` is not fully supported by a successfully read MCP documentation page in this pass; it was inferred from the official docs navigation and browser memory rather than directly verified from page content.
